hub_subtitle: Business English for HR leadership in international meetings

lesson.menu_title: This Is What I Do — Describing Your HR Role
lesson.menu_desc: Explaining your role, your team and your routine to an international colleague -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 2 -- This Is What I Do -- Your Role, Your Team, Your Routine
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Murielle Xavier | Aula 2 -- This Is What I Do

listenings.text: Hi Murielle, it's Liam from the Australia office. Welcome to the global people team. Let me explain how we work. The week usually starts with a short call on Monday morning, which sets our priorities. Recruitment and onboarding are handled by the local partners, and the policies that head office designs are implemented in every unit. We rarely make big changes on Fridays, because that day is always kept for reviews. When the workload is heavy, the tasks are shared across the team. You will work with the managers who run the Sydney office, and any process that feels too slow is quickly streamlined. It's a busy team, but a strong one. Talk soon.

Good morning, team. This is a short update from the people department. This quarter, two goals are prioritized: new talent is recruited for the operations in Chile, and our key people in Brazil are retained through better career plans. The onboarding process, which welcomes every new hire, has been streamlined, so new employees are now trained within their first week. The managers who lead each unit should send their headcount reports by Wednesday. If you have any questions that concern compensation, they are handled by your HR business partner. Thank you, and have a great week.
